entry.title: Re: RAP Draft: Side Effects for Associated Text Fields no...
entry.content:
@Boopalan 

In my understanding, the draft table stores a snapshot of all fields of the CDS entity — including association-derived fields such as DriverName and DriverMobile — as physical columns. These snapshot values are captured when the draft is created (on Edit), and are not automatically recalculated when a related FK field like DriverID is updated.

Therefore, to reflect the change on the UI immediately, you need to explicitly populate DriverName and DriverMobile in the determination as well, not just DriverID.

However, this doesn't quite align with your observation that the fields were correctly updated after refreshing the browser. Could you clarify whether that refresh was done before or after saving (activating) the draft?

entry.title: Re: I want to change the default order of the code and te...
entry.content:
Good day @keita 

Thank you for your question.

The display difference is cause by the field text arrangement. I suggest you use Adapt UI- Change Text Arrangement and set the extended field to ID and Description so that it follows the same sequence as the standard field.

If Change Text Arrangement is not available for that extended field, then the order cannot be changed through standard configuration or personalisation. In that case, I recommend the following options:

  • Use separate coded and description fields if the reporting data source supports them.
  • Extend the reporting data source through the Custom Fields app where you can and permitted to.
  • You may consider submitting an improvement request if the required text arrangement is not currently supported.

Here are references for more guidance

3669048 - Separate material number from material description from the app Material document View - SAP for Me

3696712 - Custom Fields of type Code List based on CDS Views in Analytical Apps: How to get text display option - SAP for Me

3137139 - Unable to display Cost center number without name description for exporting purposes - SAP for Me

3612451 - Custom Fields - Frequently Asked Questions - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

 

Here is a quick guide to consider:

In Display Line Items in General Ledger, enter your selection criteria, run the report, add your relevant standard field, for example Supplier. Add the extended field, for example the customer or business partner related field. Confirm the display issue in the table before you export.

In your user Profile, choose Adapt UI, right click on the table or the extended field column, check whether Change Text Arrangement is available. If available, select ID and Description. Save and activate the adaptation. Then refresh the app and export again to Excel.

If it is not available, open Custom Fields, search for the relevant custom or extended field. Check the field type and business context. Check whether the field is enabled for the relevant UI or data source. If you need, create or adjust a data source extension so that the code and description can be consumed separately in the reporting app.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Clarification on data monitor tasks when running consolidation on a yearly basis
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As part of an SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition greenfield implementation, we have a requirement where the financial consolidation using Group Reporting is expected to be executed only once after the year end closure. We have defined and assigned a new process period cycle M_YER (Fiscal Year Independent) since there's no SAP pre-delivered consolidation process period cycle for yearly consolidation. We have integrated companies as well as spreadsheet companies in our consolidation group but no currency conversion or consolidation of investments is involved. Looking for clarity on the below since there's nothing in the SAP Help documentation.

1. Since the Data Monitor inherits values from the Set Global Parameters app, it only has single Fiscal Year and Period selection and does not allow Process Year Period selection. In our scenario, where we want to execute consolidation for the entire year and not every month, would we still need to run the tasks in the Data Monitor for each of the Fiscal Year Periods individually?
E.g. Execute the tasks in the data monitor for Fiscal Year 2026 Period 1, then period 2 and so on till period 12?

2. The Consolidation Monitor (New Version) app offers the Fiscal Year Period and the Process Year Period fields. SAP documentation specifies that the process year periods group fiscal year periods for execution. When executing tasks in the Consolidation Monitor (New Version), would entering Process Year Period as 012/2026 be sufficient or would we also need to select all the Fiscal Year Period values in the Fiscal Year Period filter?

Regards,
Manas

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: SAP Results Analysis - variance release based on goods receipt proportion
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

I work in a semiconductor manufacturing company.
Our production orders typically span multiple months
(2-3 months or longer).

Current situation:
- We use SAP Material Ledger (ML) for actual costing
- Unfinished orders: variance locked in WIP until TECO
- At TECO: all accumulated variance released at once
- ML cannot match historical period movements
- Result: variance balance remains, we do manual journal entry
- Manual entry posts ALL variance to COGS regardless of inventory

My question:
Is there a standard SAP Results Analysis method that can
release WIP variance proportionally based on goods receipt
quantity each month?

For example:
- Production order: 100 units planned
- Month 1: 30 units received → release 30% variance
- Month 2: 50 units received → release 50% variance
- Month 3: 20 units received (TECO) → release 20% variance

This way ML can match variance to current period movements
and no manual journal entry needed.

Which RA method should I use? Is this possible in standard SAP?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Special G/L Indicators - Need provision to create new and rename the existing descriptions
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Special G/L Indicators available for Customer and Supplier transactions are currently delivered as part of the SAP standard configuration.

While working on multiple implementations for Indian customers, a few practical challenges are consistently observed from FI business users and finance teams:

  • The system currently does not allow creation of additional (new) Special G/L Indicators for client-specific business requirements.

  • Renaming or maintaining user-friendly descriptions for existing Special G/L Indicators is also not permitted in the Public Cloud edition.

  • Due to this limitation, FI users often face difficulty correlating the Special G/L Indicators with the relevant accounting purpose or linked G/L accounts during transaction processing, reconciliation, and reporting activities.

  • This becomes more challenging in Indian business scenarios where organizations may require additional classifications for advances, retention amounts, security deposits, statutory adjustments, or industry-specific accounting processes.

As a result, finance users need to rely heavily on internal mapping documents and user training, which increases operational dependency and the possibility of accounting errors.

Hence, similar to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there should be a provision in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to:

  1. Rename or maintain business-friendly descriptions for existing Special G/L Indicators.

  2. Create additional Special G/L Indicators based on business requirements, with appropriate configuration governance.

Such flexibility would significantly improve usability for FI users, reduce operational complexity, and support localization and industry-specific accounting practices more effectively.

entry.title: Re: One PO with Multiple GRNs and Multiple Invoices – How...
entry.content:
Below are the answers to your questions

1. Tracking invoices and payment status for a single supplier/PO
Use the PO History tab to see all Goods Receipts (GRs) and Invoice Receipts (MIRO postings) linked to the PO. Drill down into each invoice document for details. For payment status (open, partially paid, fully paid), use the Supplier Line Items app in S/4HANA Public Cloud. This combination lets you track how many invoices were raised, their linkage to the PO, and whether they are cleared or still open.

2. Managing 3‑phase payments of 33.33% each
Configure installment payment terms or a billing plan in the PO. Supplier issues three invoices, each for 33.33% of the PO value. SAP schedules and processes payments accordingly, ensuring each invoice is cleared once paid.

3. Payments linked to GR vs. higher/lower flexibility
Standard SAP behavior: Payments are tied to the quantity/value received via GR. Normally, supplier can only invoice for delivered quantities. However, it is possible to make payments higher or lower than the GR value if business requires:

  • By using down payment requests/clearing or
  • By configuring billing plans/milestone payments.
    This allows deviations from strict GR‑based invoicing when agreed contractually.

4. Handling advance or non‑proportional payments
Use Down Payment Requests (F‑47) and Down Payment Clearing (F‑54) for advance payments. Alternatively, set up milestone billing plans or partial billing plans in the PO. These options allow payments before delivery or in amounts not directly tied to GR quantities/values.

entry.title: Re: Since the WHT exemption period has expired, a warning...
entry.content:
Good day @Devi_Narasimha1 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is an expected behaviour when you configure withholding tax to be calculated at payment time. The supplier’s WHT exemption validity is checked based on the payment processing logic, so if the exemption has expired before the payment run, the system can calculate and deduct WHT during payment. Note that supplier withholding tax data includes exemption details such as certificate number, tax rate, validity period, and exemption amount.

There is no standard configuration to create a separate custom warning or error only because the supplier’s exemption validity has expired during the payment run. The available configuration controls the WHT type, WHT cod, exemption data, and whether withholding tax is realised at invoice time or payment time. Note that withholding tax type and code determine whether WHT is realised on the payment date or invoice date.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4977e7737cc747edb51d806619e3d146/5db133b41c004d97b97f31369ad4bea4.html?locale=en-US

It is recommended that you validate supplier WHT exemption validity before you run payments. If the supplier should remain exempt, correct the exemption validity in Maintain Business Partner before creating the payment proposal. If the exemption has expired legally, the WHT deduction should be allowed. If the payment proposal raises a WHT inconsistency, check and correct the supplier master and related document WHT data. See KBA 3429552 for more details.

If the supplier should still be exempt, then go back to Maintain Business Partner. Extend or correct the withholding tax exemption validity. Save the supplier. Delete the old payment proposal if needed.

 Create a new payment proposal in Manage Automatic Payments.

 Check whether WHT is no longer deducted.

If the supplier exemption has legally expired:

  1. Do not extend the exemption validity.
  2. Allow the system to calculate WHT during payment.
  3. Continue with the payment run
  4. Use the payment and WHT reporting process as required for Israel.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud] Regarding the use of BOM in order-bas...
entry.content:
Why BOM is not used with E2 is because, E2 policy group is designed for engineering/design‑based production where the product is unique to the customer order. BOM explosion is not triggered in the same way as for make‑to‑stock or standard make‑to‑order strategies. The assumption is that design work defines the structure, not a pre‑defined material BOM.

Alternatives to achieve your requirement

1) Use WBS BOMs (Project BOMs)
SAP supports WBS BOMs where components are linked directly to WBS elements. This allows dependent requirements from the final product to flow into the WBS inventory and cost objects. You can manage semi‑finished products as make‑to‑stock but still consume them via WBS BOM assignment.

2) Change Policy Group to One Supporting BOM
Instead of E2, consider E1 (Make‑to‑Order Production) or E4 (Make‑to‑Order Assembly) depending on your business scenario. These strategies allow BOM explosion and dependent requirement transfer while still keeping the make‑to‑order logic.

3) Planning Calendar / Phantom Assemblies
If semi‑finished products are standard, you can model them as phantom assemblies in the BOM. This way, the BOM is exploded during MRP, but stock is managed at semi‑finished level (make‑to‑stock). The final product remains make‑to‑order.

4) Billing/Project Integration
For project‑based manufacturing, use Project System (PS) integration. Link WBS elements to materials and costs, and assign BOMs at project level. This ensures dependent requirements flow into WBS inventory.

entry.title: What’s new in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Release for Chemical industry
entry.content:
Chemicals is a cost and profitability challenged industry. To automate and execute its business processes, an Enterprise applications solution (like SAP Cloud ERP) should cover its manufacturing complexity (continuous or batch manufacturing), execute safe and sustainable operations, ensure regulatory compliance (related to products storage, transport & their marketability) and help build a customer centric organization.

SAP Cloud ERP is a SaaS ERP with capabilities and functionalities to execute core industry specific processes. SAP Cloud ERP current coverage of Chemical industry processes is illustrated below:

I will cover in detail chemical industry relevant key enhancements and functionalities / capabilities as released in 2602 Cloud ERP in this blog and in subsequent blog.

Asset Management capabilities in 2602 Cloud ERP has been enhanced with following new functionalities:

  • Permit to Work (7Z4) enables a Chemical Enterprise to execute high risk asset maintenance jobs (like hot work) in safe and compliant manner.
  • Asset Overview is a new intuitive Fiori application which provides a comprehensive perspective / access to enterprise asset hierarchy (equipment and functional location details etc.). In the app - you can review open orders and notifications and create maintenance notifications efficiently.

2602 Cloud ERP with following new functionalities in Environment, Health and Safety enable Chemical enterprises to ensure workplace safety (for its employees), minimize environmental impact and mitigate operational risks / hazards:

  • Audit and Inspection Management (7Z7) evaluates an organization's practices, procedures, and performance related to environmental protection, workplace safety, and employee well-being with audits and site inspections
  • Occupational Health (7ZA) helps to support employee occupational healthcare. Special (healthcare) surveillance is required for employees who may be exposed to influences of a chemical, physical, or biological nature at their workplace, or who perform activities that pose a health hazard.

Following two transportation management enhancements are worth mentioning:

  • Integration of Transportation Management with Subcontracting Purchase Orders (Details). This support transportation planning, execution, and freight settlement for both outbound and inbound subcontracting (purchase orders) flows.
  • Integration of Transportation Management with Customer Consignment Orders (Details). It supports performing transportation planning, execution, and freight costs settlement for customer consignment fill-up and pickup orders

In the next blog I will cover enhancements in Product Compliance and Sales.

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud] Regarding the use of BOM in order-bas...
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

In Public Cloud E2 should not be used if the business expectation is that the sales order for the finished product will explode the BOM and automatically create dependent requirements for the WBS/project stock. E2 is the planning strategy Engineer to Order: Sales from Project Stock Without Planning, so its purpose is sales from project stock, not BOM-driven sales-order planning. SAP also confirms for Public Cloud that sales delivery from project stock is supported through scope item 6GD with strategy group E2 and a WBS element assigned to the sales order item

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/57fcbf5d6d1f484db58a2df42be4f63d.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3651125

Therefore there is no standard setting that makes E2 behave like a normal BOM-exploding make-to-order strategy. The BOM can still exist as master data but E2 should not be positioned as the mechanism to generate the full dependent-requirement structure from the finished-product sales order

The recommended design is to split the requirement:

1. Use E2 / scope item 6GD for the sales process where the final product is delivered from project stock

2. Plan the semi-finished materials through project/WBS-level material demand for example by using Manage Project Demand / Project Control Procurement where applicable. This keeps the requirement at WBS/project-stock level and allows the project demand process to use BOM explosion functionality where supported by the selected demand type and BOM explosion settings, instead of relying on sales-order BOM explosion from E2. This keeps WBS stock / Special Stock Q and WBS cost control as the leading design

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6b39bd1d0e5e4099a5b65d835c29c696/b6c10ebe86a94eed953889a37e8ff565.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/897281e65b984732bc818f7a556cfd62.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/852df7e0a5d748deb52b1dc9c2baa4e0.html

3. If the key requirement is instead to explode the finished-product BOM directly from the sales order then use a normal make-to-order strategy such as S5 Make-to-Order Production with Costing. SAP Help explicitly states that S5 supports sales order BOM processing and that a BOM is required for the finished product

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/40d700f2876d4de8832c0050f8fd7940.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Create Custom Search View and Custom Result View SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
To Integrate the Custom Search View and Result view to the Navigation Bar:

  • Go to spro->Define Work Area Component Repository.
  • Give the Component Name, Window Name and go to Inbound Plug Definition and give the Target Id and other details.
  • In TCode crmc_ui_nblinks, create the logical link with the Target ID and assign this logical link to work center group link.
  • The Work Center Group link was assigned to a Work Center and Work center to a Navigation Bar Profile.
  • Then in TCode CRMC_UI_NBLINKS the Navigation Bar Profile was assigned to a Business Role and the Work Center Group Links and Work Center was adjusted to make them visible.
  • Then in TCode ppoma_crm, the Business role was assigned to position.
  • By this all users assigned to the position will have access to the Business Role ->Navigation Bar Profile -> Work Center -> Work Center group link -> Logical Link.

entry.title: Automatic payment block required for employee invoices when higher-value credit notes exist
entry.content:
We have a business requirement from our Germany team related to the processing of employee expense invoices and credit notes in SAP.

Currently, employee credit notes are often posted with a significantly higher value than the corresponding employee expense invoices. However, the employee expense invoices are posted with immediate payment terms and comparatively lower invoice amounts.

As a result, during the payment run:

  • The employee expense invoices are picked up and paid immediately.
  • The higher-value employee credit notes remain open and move to the exception queue due to insufficient offsetting/open item balancing.

This creates operational and reconciliation challenges, as the related invoice and credit note are not processed together.

entry.title: User Provisioning for SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
User Provisioning from SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) to SAP Fieldglass using Identity Provisioning Services (IPS)

 

Introduction

SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) is SAP's identity management platform comprising four key components:

  1. Identity Authentication Service (IAS) - Handles user authentication, identity storage, and user management.
  2. Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) - Automates user provisioning and synchronization across connected systems.
  3. Identity Directory - Acts as the central user directory for storing and managing identities and groups.
  4. Authorization and Trust Management Service - Manages application authorizations, role collections, and trust configurations across SAP solutions.

For this scenario, we will primarily focus on:

  • IAS for user creation and management
  • IPS for provisioning users into SAP Fieldglass

SAP Fieldglass is SAP's cloud-based Vendor Management System (VMS) used to manage external workforce, contractors, and services procurement.

Note: SAP Fieldglass comes with a preconfigured source and target systems. You can use them as-is and run provisioning jobs.

Problem Statement -

Organizations managing users centrally in SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS/IAS) often face provisioning failures while provisioning users to SAP Fieldglass due to missing mandatory fields, incorrect Business Unit mappings, and IPS transformation issues.

Proposed Solution -

This blog provides a step-by-step guide for provisioning users from SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS/IAS) to SAP Fieldglass using SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS). It covers system configuration, attribute and Business Unit mapping validation, provisioning job execution, and troubleshooting common provisioning issues to ensure successful user synchronization.

Note: In this blog, CIS (Cloud Identity Services) and IAS (Identity Authentication Service) may be used interchangeably, as IAS is rebranded as SAP CIS (Cloud Identity Services).

In many SAP landscapes, users are created and managed centrally in CIS and then provisioned to downstream systems such as SAP Fieldglass. This blog walks through the end-to-end process of provisioning users from CIS to SAP Fieldglass using Identity Provisioning Service (IPS).

---

Provisioning Architecture

The user provisioning flow in this scenario is:

CIS (Source System) → IPS (Provisioning Service) → SAP Fieldglass (Target System)

Flow Overview:

  • Users are created in CIS/IAS
  • IPS reads users from CIS/IAS
  • IPS applies transformations and attribute mappings
  • Users are provisioned into SAP Fieldglass

This setup enables centralized identity management across the SAP landscape.

---

Step 1: Understand the Mandatory Field Requirement

Before creating users or running provisioning jobs, it is important to understand one of the most common causes of provisioning failures in SAP Fieldglass.

The following fields are mandatory for successful user provisioning:

  1. First Name
  2. Login Name
  3. Primary Business Unit

NOTE: If these fields are not populated correctly, user provisioning to Fieldglass will fail.

In many implementations, the Primary Business Unit is mapped from another CIS attribute through IPS transformations.

For example, in below case:

CIS – ‘Division’ → Fieldglass Primary Business Unit

This means the CIS Division field will act as the Primary Business Unit during provisioning.

---

Step 2: Verify the Primary Business Unit Mapping

To identify which CIS field is mapped to the Primary Business Unit in Fieldglass, navigate to:

Identity Provisioning → Target System → Transformations

Review the transformation mappings and check which fields are marked as mandatory or non-optional.

In this example: CIS – ‘Division’ is mapped to the Fieldglass Primary Business Unit

This step is extremely important while troubleshooting provisioning failures.

---

Step 3: Verify Valid Business Unit Codes in Fieldglass

The Primary Business Unit value used during provisioning must already exist in SAP Fieldglass. The Business Unit Code from Fieldglass is the exact value that must be populated in the CIS/IAS field mapped to the Primary Business Unit.

To verify the available Business Unit codes:

  1. Log in to the SAP Fieldglass landscape with admin access
  2. Open the Admin Console (bottom-left tab)
  3. Navigate to: Company Structure → Business Unit
  4. Refer to the Code column

The values under the Code column represent the valid Business Unit codes configured in Fieldglass.

Example: FINANCE

Below image shows the ‘Code’ column:

The value entered in the CIS/IAS – ‘Division’ field must exactly match one of these codes:

Example - CIS/IAS Division = FINANCE

IMPORTANT: The value is case-sensitive and must match exactly with the Business Unit code configured in Fieldglass.

---

Step 4: Create the User in CIS/IAS=

Navigate to your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant:

User & Authorizations → User Management → Add 

Below you can find the mandatory fields highlighted.

While creating the user, fill in as many fields as possible. Most importantly ensure:

  • First Name is populated
  • Login Name is populated
  • The mapped Business Unit field is populated with the exact Fieldglass Business Unit code

In this example:

Division = FINANCE

IMPORTANT: The value must match exactly with the Business Unit code configured in Fieldglass.

---

Step 5: Run the Identity Provisioning Job

Navigate to:

Identity Provisioning → Source System → Jobs

You may see multiple provisioning job options depending on your IPS configuration and system type:

Job Type

Description

Read Job

Processes only newly changed or updated records from the source system

Resync Job               

Processes all record again to fully synchronize the target system with the source

Simulate Job             

Simulates the provisioning process without writing data to the target system

Validate Job

Used for testing attribute mappings, filters, and custom logic.

 

When to Use Each Job:

  • Use a Read Job for incremental updates or newly added users
  • Use a Resync Job during initial migrations, mapping corrections, or troubleshooting scenarios
  • Use a Simulate Job to validate mappings and transformations before actual provisioning
  • Use a Validate Job to verify attribute mappings, filters, and transformation logic before running actual provisioning or synchronization jobs

For this migration scenario, provisioning is typically performed using either a ‘Read Job’ or a ‘Resync Job’ depending on the requirement. Select Run Job.

---

Step 6: Verify Provisioning Logs

Once the provisioning job is completed, navigate to:

Identity Provisioning → Provisioning Logs

Here you can verify:

  • Successful user transfers
  • Failed provisioning attempts
  • Missing mandatory attributes
  • Mapping issues
  • Synchronization errors

Provisioning Logs are the primary troubleshooting area for IPS migrations.

---

Step 7: Verify Users in SAP Fieldglass

As a final validation step, log in to SAP Fieldglass with admin access and navigate to:

Admin Console → User → Under ‘User List’ you can see all the users.

You should now be able to see all successfully provisioned users listed.

 

 

This confirms that the provisioning process has completed successfully.

---

Common Issues and Troubleshooting

 

1. Missing First Name or Login Name

Issue: Provisioning fails because the First Name or Login Name field is empty.

Resolution: Populate both fields in CIS/IAS before running the provisioning job.

 

2. Invalid Business Unit

Issue: The Business Unit value provided in CIS/IAS does not exist in Fieldglass.

Resolution: Verify valid Business Unit codes under:

Admin Console → Company Structure → Business Unit

Use the exact value from the Code column.

 

3. Incorrect Attribute Mapping

Issue: The wrong CIS/IAS attribute is mapped to the Fieldglass Primary Business Unit.

Resolution: Review the IPS transformation mappings under:

Target System → Transformations

 

4. Provisioning Job Completes Successfully but User Is Not Visible

Possible Causes: Missing mandatory fields, invalid Business Unit code, incorrect transformation mapping, or IPS filtering configuration issues.

Resolution: Review the Provisioning Logs carefully and validate all mandatory attributes and mappings.

---

Key Takeaways

  • Always populate First Name, Login Name, and Primary Business Unit before provisioning users
  • Verify which CIS attribute maps to the Fieldglass Primary Business Unit
  • Use valid Business Unit codes from the Fieldglass Admin Console
  • Review IPS transformation mappings carefully
  • Monitor Provisioning Logs after every job execution

---

Conclusion

Provisioning users from SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS/IAS) to SAP Fieldglass using Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) is a straightforward process when the mandatory field requirements and transformation mappings are configured correctly.

By validating Business Unit mappings, maintaining correct attribute values, and monitoring IPS provisioning logs, organizations can seamlessly synchronize users into SAP Fieldglass and streamline identity management across their SAP landscape.

---

Screenshot Disclaimer

Screenshots may vary depending on SAP version, configuration, roles, and UI updates; use the navigation paths and concepts.as the primary reference.

entry.title: Re: Regarding SAP GRC
entry.content:
Hello @Go-kul_29 ,

SAP GRC is a strong long-term career path, especially for someone with your technical background. The best approach is to first build a solid foundation in SAP Security and authorizations, then specialize in SAP GRC Access Control, risk analysis, and compliance workflows. Focus on hands-on practice with roles, users, SoD concepts, and workflows, because companies value practical troubleshooting skills much more than theory alone.

 

TopicGuidance
Is SAP GRC a good career?✔ Yes. SAP GRC (Governance, Risk & Compliance) is a strong niche with steady demand because companies need security, audit, compliance, and SoD (Segregation of Duties) controls for SAP systems.
Your background advantageYour Computer Science + AI/ML background is actually very useful because SAP GRC combines: ✔ Security ✔ Access control ✔ Risk analysis ✔ Process governance
Best starting pointStart with SAP Security + SAP GRC Access Control. This is the most common entry path into GRC consulting.
Key SAP GRC areas to learn✔ Access Control (AC) ✔ Process Control (PC) ✔ Risk Management (RM) ✔ Emergency Access Management (Firefighter IDs) ✔ User provisioning
Most important concepts✔ Segregation of Duties (SoD) ✔ Role design ✔ User provisioning ✔ Risk analysis ✔ SAP authorization objects
Recommended first certificationStart with SAP Security/GRC-related learning on: ✔ SAP Learning Hub
Should you learn SAP Basis?Basic understanding helps, but you do NOT need deep Basis administration initially.
Hands-on practice✔ Practice SU01, PFCG, SUIM, role creation, firefighter setup, risk analysis scenarios.
Important SAP transactions✔ SU01 – User management ✔ PFCG – Role maintenance ✔ SUIM – User/authorization reporting
Best way to become job-readyLearn both: ✔ Functional concepts (risk/compliance) ✔ Technical concepts (roles/authorizations/workflows)
Entry-level roles✔ SAP Security Analyst ✔ SAP GRC Analyst ✔ SAP Access Control Support ✔ IAM (Identity Access Management) Associate
Market demandStrong in: ✔ Consulting firms ✔ Big 4 ✔ Manufacturing MNCs ✔ Banking & pharma
Future outlookVery stable long-term because compliance and cybersecurity requirements continue growing, especially in S/4HANA and cloud environments.
AI/ML relevanceYour AI/ML background can help later in: ✔ Risk analytics ✔ Anomaly detection ✔ Intelligent access monitoring
Biggest beginner mistakeFocusing only on SAP screens without understanding: ✔ Business risks ✔ Audit concepts ✔ Compliance controls


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud User and Team Provisioning SCIM API Best Practices and Sample Scripts
entry.content:
Dear Sayedzubair

You need to use multiple sample scripts, and I would recommend using the dormant users scenario as a basis for what you want to do, it might even meet your requirements fully.  The dormant users scenario uses the 'admin tool kit' (sample script 1665), which can identify which users have public and private content. However, there are some limitations to this, documented in my user guide. You'll find a number of sample data files with the admintoolkit, making it easy to get going.

Dormant users scenario: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-managing-dormant-users/ba-p/13578921

A few of the sample data files for 1665 are:

x665 sample - 20 - AdminToolKit_Users_Created_Over_A_Year_Ago.json
x665 sample - 21 - AdminToolKit_Users_Created_Recently.json
x665 sample - 22 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_A_Recent_Login.json
x665 sample - 23 - AdminToolKit_Users_Without_A_Recent_Login.json
x665 sample - 24 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_Few_Logins_Since_Oldest_Log_Entry.json
x665 sample - 25 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_Few_Logins_This_Last_Month.json
x665 sample - 26 - AdminToolKit_Users_Without_A_Recent_Login_And_Fewer_Than_3_Logins_Last_3_Months.json
x665 sample - 27 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_100_Or_More_Logins_Since_Oldest_Log_Entry.json
x665 sample - 28 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_100_Or_More_Logins_Within_Last_3_Months.json
x665 sample - 29 - AdminToolKit_Users_With_Private_Folder_Content.json
x665 sample - 30 - AdminToolKit_Users_Without_Private_Folder_Content.json
x665 sample - 31 - AdminToolKit_Users_That_Created_Public_Folder_Content.json
x665 sample - 32 - AdminToolKit_Users_That_Did_Not_Create_Public_Folder_Content.json

entry.title: Re: How do you grant HRBP access to Performance Forms?
entry.content:
With the Latest Profile, we had to do the step below to allow the access via the Profile again, hopefully this helps.

2614402 - Configuring the Performance History Portlet in Performance Management - SAP for Me: To allow users to open all forms that are listed in Performance History, you must select Allow users to view details of forms that have never been in their Inbox in Performance Management Feature Settings. Otherwise, they can only access forms that have been in their Inbox at least once.

entry.title: Re: Post parked documents
entry.content:
Hello @Linyi_Yang ,

In standard SAP S/4HANA behavior, parked documents are generally posted from the document detail/edit screen rather than directly from the item list view. SAP does this to ensure validations, workflow checks, and authorization controls are executed properly. If your business requires direct posting from the list view, it would typically require a custom Fiori enhancement or UI extension.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Launch SAP GUI Transactions from SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
To Auto populate the Sold To Party number when the ECC Transaction VA01 is launched from Web UI below are the steps:

  • Go to TCode SWELS and click on Start Trace.
  • Run the TCode VA01 and go to TCode SWELS and click on End Trace.
  • Go to TCode SWEL and get the Business Object.
  • Business Object for TCode VA01 is BUS2032.
  • Create ZBUS2032 as a copy of BUS2032 as the Business Object BUS2032 does not have Sold-To-Party as a Key Field.
  • Add a New method to call the VA01 TCode.
  • Change the status of method to Implemented and Released.
  • Change the status of the Business Object to Implemented and Released.
  • Utilize this Business Object in the Transaction Launcher Wizard (Transaction Launcher Type BOR Transaction).

entry.title: How to extend ABAP Test Cockpit on SAP BTP for custom use cases
entry.content:
If you are using the ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) on SAP BTP already as a central ATC check system for developments in your private cloud or on-premise systems, and have ideas like

  • I want to visualize ATC findings on my own quality dashboard
  • I want to setup approver areas programmatically
  • I want to enhance exemption flow
  • I want to programmatically evaluate ATC findings
  • .... or do something else

then this blog post is for you to guide you which released ATC APIs and CDS views are available for your extension use case.  

If you have any additional use cases in mind, please let us know as comment on this blog post.

 

Released APIs for ATC on SAP BTP

The released APIs (C1 contract, “For Use in Cloud Development”) for ATC on SAP BTP support different use cases like starting local ATC runs, processing ATC exemptions, configuring ATC approver responsibilities or implementing custom ATC checks.

The single entry point for the released ATC APIs is the class CL_SATC_API. The method CL_SATC_API=>CREATE_API_FACTORY returns a factory object from which specialized sub￼factories can be instantiated for different use cases. In what follows, whenever a code snippet mentions an object named ATC, it refers to this API factory. We therefore assume:

data(atc) = cl_satc_api=>create_api_factory( ).

The following describes how these APIs support different use cases.

Read ATC results

An ATC run result consists of a header and a list of findings. The headers of an ATC run result are accessible via the CDS view SATC_API_RESULT_HEADERS. The key ResultId is a GUID that uniquely identifies this ATC result. While the header provides general information of the check run (title, completion time), the actual findings are accessible via the CDS view SATC_API_FINDINGS. Findings belong to an ATC run if their ResultId matches the run's header ResultId.

Alternatively, the API factory also offers access to the findings of an ATC run via 

data(result) = atc->get_result_by_id( result_id ).

This approach includes additional information not available through direct CDS view queries, such as the individual texts of the findings.

Start ATC runs

This API allows you to start local ATC runs for transports and object lists. These runs are always synchronous - they execute in the session in which they are started and do not run as background jobs. Results are available only through the API; these runs are not custom code analysis projects and do not appear in the Analyze Custom Code app.

data(objects_to_check) = atc->create_object_set_fr_transport( 'ATCK000001' ).
data(run_config) = atc->create_run_configuration( objects_to_check ).
data(run) = atc->create_run( run_config ).
data(result) = run->run( )->get_findings_with_text( ).

Approve/reject ATC exemptions

Both types of exemptions can be approved or rejected programmatically using the exemption controller:

data(xmpt_controller) = atc->get_exemption_controller( ).
data(ids_and_assessments) = value if_satc_exemption_controller=>ty_exemptions_eval(
  ( exemption_id = exemption_id assessment = `Approved, will be fixed in a future release.` ) ).
data(error_messages) = xmpt_controller->approve_exemptions_by_id( ids_and_assessments ).

If the user lacks the required authorizations or another error occurs, the approval/rejection step returns an error.

Configure approver responsibilities

While there is currently no UI in the SAP BTP ABAP environment systems to configure fine-grained approver responsibilities, these can be activated and configured through the API. The read and change APIs provide access to all required parts, including defining check and object groups, defining areas of responsibility, and assigning specific users to those areas.

data(appr_resp_config) = atc->get_approver_resp_api_for_chng( ).
appr_resp_config->set_enabled( abap_true ).

data(object_group_config) = appr_resp_config->get_object_group_api_for_chng( ).
object_group_config->create_groups( value #( 
  ( name = 'MY_OBJECTS' 
    description = 'My object group' 
    range = value #( 
      ( category = if_satc_api_appr_object_grps_r=>category-object_name
        sign = if_satc_api_appr_sel_criteria=>sign-include
        option = if_satc_api_appr_sel_criteria=>option-contains_pattern
        value = 'Z_MY_OBJECT_*' ) ) )
  ) ).
object_group_config->save( ).

data(check_group_config) = appr_resp_config->get_check_group_api_for_change( ).
check_group_config->create_groups( value #(
  ( name = 'ALL_CHECKS'
    description = 'Every check'
    range = value #( 
      ( category = if_satc_api_appr_check_grps_r=>category-check_name 
        sign = if_satc_api_appr_sel_criteria=>sign-include 
        option = if_satc_api_appr_sel_criteria=>option-contains_pattern 
        value = '*' ) ) ) 
  ) ).
check_group_config->save( ).

data(area_config) = appr_resp_config->get_area_api_for_change( ).
area_config->create_areas( value #(
  ( name = 'MY_AREA' 
    description = 'My objects'
    object_group = 'MY_OBJECTS'
    message_group = 'ALL_CHECKS' ) 
  ) ).
area_config->save( ).
  
data(user_config) = appr_resp_config->get_resp_api_for_change( ).
user_config->create_area_assignments( value #( 
  ( area = 'MY_AREA' approver = sy-uname )
  ) ).
user_config->save( ).

appr_resp_config->save( ).

This code creates an object group named MY_OBJECTS, a message group named ALL_CHECKS, and an area named MY_AREA comprising those groups. It then assigns the current user to that area.

Implement custom ATC check

The released API IF_CI_ATC_CHECK provides you the opportunity to implement your own custom ATC check. See the blog post  How to create your own ATC check in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse for details.

 

Released CDS views to access ATC data

For some read-only use cases, there are also released CDS views that can be used to access ATC data.

You can get the overview of available released CDS views either in the ABAP development tools for Eclipse by applying the search filter api:use_in_cloud_development appl:bc-dwb-cex-ccm, bc-dwb-too-atf type:ddls:

or in the Cloudification Repository: https://sap.github.io/abap-atc-cr-cv-s4hc/?product=btp&states=released&softwareComponents=SAP_BASIS&applicationComponents=BC-DWB-CEX-CCM%2CBC-DWB-TOO-ATF&objectTypes=CDS_STOB

Get ATC findings

The CDS view SATC_API_FINDINGS returns ATC check run result findings with basic information about the affected object, potential exemption information, location and information about the check which produced the finding.

Get ATC results headers

The CDS view SATC_API_RESULT_HEADERS returns ATC check run results with aggregated basic information about the number of findings with certain priorities, potential number of check run failures and some scheduling information of the check run.

Read ATC exemptions

You can use the CDS view SATC_EXEMPTIONS_DDLV_EC1 to retrieve central exemptions and the CDS view SATC_EXEMPTIONS_DDLV_EC2 to retrieve local exemptions (object type CHKE).

Access ATC results of the Custom Code Migration app projects

If you want to access ATC results which have been created in the Analyze Custom Code app (Custom Code Migration app), you can use the CDS views SYCM_APS_PROJECT and SYCM_APS_ATC_FINDINGS. The findings view is also linked to the CDS view SATC_API_FINDINGS mentioned above.

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 4)
entry.content:
Hello #SAPCommunity,

My name is Sergio Guerrero, and I am a SAP #HANA /#Fiori developer, 2x book author - one on Building Fiori apps and one on Building microservices in XSA . I have always been fascinated by software and I am always looking at ways to improve processes and procedures in software as well as in day-to-day life. I started my journey in SAP HANA back in 2013 doing SAPUI5 development, and with time, I have worked in Fiori as well as in HANA modeling. I focus on improving performance in data modeling and always looking at ways to marry those data models to end user applications, such as Fiori apps.

My experience in SAP HANA / Fiori expands across various industries, such as, Industrial, Retail, Oil & Gas, Defense. I enjoy participating in #SAPCommunity events such as #SAPInsideTrack, #CodeJams, #Stammtisch and any of the #TechEd / #TechConnect conferences I can attend & meet other like-minded individuals; and sometimes even play some #pickleball😉.

Due to my involvement with the #SAPChampion program, I am participating in the session 4 of AMA (Ask Me Anything) series. The AMA series allows community members to ask questions related to specific topics. My session is dedicated to Fiori applications (sapui5, Fiori-like apps on HANA XSA, CAP, or S/4) and I welcome questions related to this topic. If you prefer anonymous questions, please submit them before June30th, 2006 -> here.

If you don't have questions, say hi anyways and share your learning with others. Hope we can meet at one of the near future events!

cheers!

entry.title: Re: Developing for Analytics with the SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi @rfreitas87 and @MarioLenz,

first of all, I am really sorry that I missed answering the question earlier 🙁.

Yes, in the meantime, we do also have the option to create native Fiori apps using CDS based analytical queries. The clients are described in the ABAP Analytics Developer Guide. In fact, we have multiple options already, using the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) with generator wizards without the need to go to Business Application Studio:

Option 1) is building a so called "Multidimensional Analysis Application" (MDA). The MDA App can display one analytical query at a time. There is a YouTube video for the development process here: YouTube video on MDA 

Option 2) is building a "Review Booklet Application" (RVB). The Review Booklet app can display multiple queries in one UI and can be personalized a lot more in detail (e.g. by defining the visualization as chart or defining the layout in more detail). We also did a session on that on YouTube that explains the whole process: YouTube video on RVB

Hope this helps. Maybe I'll do an extra blog post on the options in the future as well.

Best Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: Re: SAP Results Analysis - variance release based on good...
entry.content:
No, SAP does not provide a standard Results Analysis (RA) method that proportionally releases production order variance based on monthly goods receipt quantities. In standard Material Ledger, variances are only released at TECO (order closure). To achieve proportional release, you would need to use alternative approaches such as Product Cost by Period with cost collectors, or project‑based RA methods (POC, cost‑based) — but these are not directly applicable to production orders.

RA is designed for projects and sales orders, not production orders. Common methods include:

  • Revenue‑based (POC): Recognizes costs/revenues proportionally to progress.
  • Cost‑based: Recognizes WIP based on actual costs incurred.
  • Completed contract: Defers recognition until project completion.

These methods are not available for production orders in ML; they apply to WBS elements or sales orders.

Alternatives you can consider

1) Product Cost by Period (Cost Collectors)
Instead of long production orders, use product cost collectors. Variances are calculated and settled periodically, not only at TECO. This allows proportional variance allocation aligned with monthly GRs.

2) Split Orders or Periodic Closing
Break long production orders into smaller monthly orders. Each order closes monthly, releasing variance proportionally. This avoids manual journal entries but increases order volume.

3) Project System Integration (WBS + RA)
If production is project‑driven, assign costs to WBS elements. Use RA methods (cost‑based or POC) to recognize WIP/variance proportionally. Settlement posts to FI/CO each period.

4) Custom Enhancement
Some companies build a custom allocation logic to spread variance based on GR quantities. This is not standard but can automate what you currently do manually.

entry.title: Your Questions Answered: Managing Certificates in SAP Business One
entry.content:
What a great start to our 2026 Partner Enablement series! So far this May, the SAP Business One Support Organization has hosted three webinars, and the response has been phenomenal. With nearly 600 partners joining us live from around the globe, we are truly humbled, surprised, and encouraged by your engagement and eagerness to learn. 

The series was created to share practical knowledge built from the real-world cases and common issues we see every day. During these live events, the Q&A portion is often as insightful as the presentation itself, but these valuable discussions don't always make it into the final recordings. 

That’s why we’re here today. To ensure everyone benefits, we've compiled all the questions asked during our recent Session #10 - Managing certificates in SAP Business One. 

If you missed the session or want a refresher, you can find the recording and materials for all ten delivered sessions in our central KBA: 3525031 - Central KBA for Partner Enablement Sessions. 

Now, let's dive into your questions. 

Q&A

Q: How is SAP preparing now that SSL is jumping on a stage where certificates will last (even) less than 100 days at some point? 

A: Solution management is evaluating current options.  

 

Q: Are certificates used with developed Add-ons? 

A: It depends what technology is used. SL requires a valid certificate while DI API and UI API do not require it.  

 

Q: Is there a way to update it via .p12 on the SAP business One on prem  directory  not reconfiguration?  Like SAP Business One Browser Access service you can easily update it by overwriting the certificate then restart the services SAP BAS then it will be renewed.  

A: Certificates can be updated in all necessary places with the reconfiguration wizard or manually in each component. 

 

Q: SAP Business One mobile app need ssl certificate? Does Ssl certificate need vpn to be working? 

A: VPN are 3rd party product so this question is rather for VPN providers. Mobile apps require a valid certificate. 

 

Q: How many levels need to be present in a chain? 

A: This is question for CA authorities. 

 

Q: Will we be able to use certificates that have a valid period of more then 2 years? 

A: The conditions for valid certificates are defined by CA authorities. Current trend is shortening the validity period. 

 

Q: Why is service layer ssl in form of (crt  , key) instead of using keystore like sld service, is it related to using apache httpd vs tomcat? 

A: Service layer is build with different technology, it is not a java solution like SLD. 

 

Q: Can we use different certificates for different SAP components? ex. Service Layer different than SLD. 

A: Reconfiguration wizard allows you to import only one certificate. Having different certificates for different B1 services would require manual certificate installation. 

 

Q: Can SSL expiration be captured from the RSP studio? 

A: It would require to build an own scenario. 

 

Q: Do you have more information in notes where the actions are listed to properly provide certificates in order to enable certificate validation?  

A: We have several notes regarding certificates, most of them are mentioned in this session presentation. Please see KBA above for slides.  

 

Q: Is there a way to update the ssl of SLD and Authentication Service without reconfiguring SAP Business One Server Components? Like overwriting the file then restart the services.  

A: Manual certificate installation.  

 

Q: Can we use SBO-mailer to email out alert/warnings about SSL expiry dates? 

A: There is no such a functionality at the moment.  

 

Q: Will SAP implement support for Let’s Encrypt or similar services? 

Q: There's a way to auto-renew certificates within the valid period or use Let's Encrypt? using any tool like certbot, acme, etc? 

A: The possible solutions are evaluated by the solution management. 

 

Q: On HANA, you mentioned that all SAP components must be installed on FQDN - So I have an instance where that is done, however the HANA Hostname is different but the DNS is added to the SAN but it gives validity issues, do you recommend we build the HANA box with the DNS rather? 

A: Hana server also have to have FQDN. 

What's Next? Your Guide to SAP Business One Support Resources 

We hope this Q&A provides the clarity you were looking for. As we continue our webinar series, we are already in the early stages of preparing for our next topic: a deep dive into the Service Layer. This highly anticipated session will be delivered by the expert SDK team under Product Area Lead Balaji Sivaramakrishnan. This is for partners and partner register for free here → SAP Partner Portal | Upcoming Partner Session Delivered by SAP Business One Support Team

In the meantime, don’t forget to leverage these valuable support channels to find answers and stay connected: 

  • Guided Answers: Need step-by-step help? SAP Business One Support has now released 31 different Guided Answers to walk you through common processes. Find them all linked in the central KBA: 3213852 - Central KBA for SAP Business One Guided Answers. 
  • The Support Spotlight Blogs: Looking for more tips, tricks, and deep dives from our team? Find our full collection of articles here: Tips from SAP Business One Support. 
  • SAP Business One Community: Have a question or want to share your expertise? Keep the conversation going and interact with peers and experts in the  SAP Business One Community. 

Thank you for your continued partnership and engagement. We look forward to seeing you at the next session

entry.title: Re: RKANBU01 - Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @XANDOGAN,

No in Public Cloud there is no released application or standard application job that is equivalent to the classic report RKANBU01

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0001746429

In Public Cloud this type of classic correction report is not available for customer execution as a released job template. Application job templates are based on SAP-delivered job catalog entries and parameters so a classic ABAP correction report cannot be used unless SAP explicitly delivers it as a released Public Cloud job template

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/9c930d50f09e45f8b7f516bde4a8e96d.html

The Public Cloud function Periodic Commitment Adjustments should not be treated as an RKANBU01 replacement. It is related to Public Sector commitment processing such as closing, reassignment or carry-forward of commitments, not correction of PR/PO commitment inconsistencies

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/93bd0b1e72ca4cbcbfb942d4497529f7/fbc655f34936497ab60d07ec66d00ee9.html

Recommended approach:

If the issue is a real commitment inconsistency in Public Cloud and it cannot be resolved through the standard business document flow the correct route is to raise an SAP incident with concrete examples such as PR/PO number, WBS/cost center/internal order, company code, fiscal year and the affected commitment values BUT before opening the SAP incident the reported values should first be validated in the relevant standard apps like Project Cost Report Overview / Project Budget Report for WBS-related commitments and Commitments by Cost Center for cost center-related commitments

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2513')/S36

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3016')/S36

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to delimit recurring pay components after employe...
entry.content:
Usually there is no need to delimit recurring payments with the termination, as payroll typically will take into account only the (part- ) periods prior to the leaving. 

It still might be helpful to remove future dated records, which had been created prior to the employee leaving. With 2026 H1 release, there is a new standard functionality for automated deletion of future dated records after termination, please see Automated Deletion of Future-Dated Job and Compensation Information Records After Employment Termination | SAP Help Portal

In order to delimit all recurring pay a custom solution as described in this blog Managing Recurring Pay Components in SuccessFactors Using Batch Upsert might be created.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release Now Live - Resources and Information
entry.content:
The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release is now live in production. With new innovations and enhancements delivered across the suite, the next step is understanding what’s new and what’s relevant for your landscape.

We’ve put together a range of resources that can help you quickly get up to speed.

The sections below outline how to navigate those resources and make sense of the 1H 2026 release.

                                                                                                                                                                                      

How to Navigate the 1H 2026 Release

As with every release, there’s a lot of content available. A good starting point is the combination of the release brochure and overview video, which together provide a quick look at key highlights.

From there, the What’s New Viewer becomes the main reference point. This is where you can explore updates in more detail, understand what’s automatically applied versus configurable, and identify anything that may require closer attention.

If you want to see new features in action, the short demos help make everything more tangible. For a broader and more in-depth view, the solution walkthrough videos take a deeper dive into key updates within a given solution area, bringing together multiple innovations and showing how they work in practice.

Finally, the product area blogs and on-demand webinars, such as the Product Q&A and Strategic Product Road Map Update, add additional context.

                                                                                                                                                                                       

Solution Walkthrough Videos (now available!)

We just launched a new set of solution walkthrough videos that provide a deeper look at key updates by solution area.

These videos highlight multiple innovations within each solution area and show how they work in practice.

You can explore them here:

  • Employee Central
  • Employee Central Payroll
  • Enterprise Service Management
  • Talent Management
  • SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Learning
  • People Intelligence
  • Platform & System Administration

                                                                                                                                                                                              

Bringing It All Together

To simplify navigation, all of these resources are available on the Product Release and Road Map page in the SAP Community.

                                                                                                                                                                                          

Looking Ahead

Beyond the individual updates, the 1H 2026 release also reflects the broader direction shared at SAP Sapphire.

Many of the innovations in this release tie into SAP’s vision for the Autonomous Enterprise, where AI is embedded directly into business processes to not just support decisions, but increasingly help execute them.

If you haven’t already, it’s worth catching up on the recent SAP Sapphire sessions and announcements to see how this release fits into that broader strategy.

entry.title: Re: Report showing all assets including those with no tra...
entry.content:
Hello @kezelda 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you are correct in your understanding. Custom Analytical Queries and Services cannot be use to freely join multiple sources directly inside the query. The query needs an analytical data source. See KBA 3227982 for more details on the creation of custom CDS view with an analytical scenario and its dependencies.

The main limitation is that I_FixedAsset is a master data/dimension type source. It is suitable for listing all assets, including assets with no postings, but it cannot be used as the primary source for an analytical cube. See KBA 2797850 for more details.

I suggest you separate the requirement into two reporting layers. Use an Analytical Dimension based on I_FixedAsset for the complete asset master list, including inactive transaction assets and location where available. Use Asset Balances for balance and amount reporting, because this app is designed for fixed asset balance values by company code, depreciation area, segment, and profit center. 

If your business needs one single report that starts from all assets and also shows balances, including zero transaction assets, you cannot fully achieve it with standard key user analytical modelling tools. The next step would either be to combine all the assets list and Asset Balances output outside the embedded analytical query, or you may raise an improvement request for a standard reporting enhancement.

See the following reference for more details.

3303477 - Error "View must contain at least one measure" when creating a custom CDS view of type Analytical Cube - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: ISU Billing a meter with 3 registers
entry.content:
Hi @ricky_shaw 

We don’t define the time slices inside the TOU route. They are defined inside the rate itself, but only when the rate is configured as a TOU‑based rate category.

In EA30, the “Time-of-Use / Time Slices” option only appears when:

  • The rate category is marked as TOU‑relevant, and (EA30 → Rate Category->TOU Indicator” / “TOU-Relevant Billing) if you can't see it you might be in lower version. 

  • The rate steps are configured to use TOU logic.

If you don’t see the Time Slice section, it means the rate category is not set up as TOU. Once the rate category is TOU‑enabled, EA30 will show the Time Slice node automatically.

So yes — you must choose a TOU‑enabled rate category, not the TOU route. The TOU route is only for meter reading scheduling, not billing logic.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Governance: A CSM Perspective
entry.content:
Why Some HR Transformations Succeed, while Others don’t

Many organizations invest in cloud HR platforms like SAP SuccessFactors with similar ideas in mind and maybe using similar methodology, yet the outcomes can look completely different.

Some months after the go live, when the inevitable stabilization period ends, some organizations unlock real business value, improve employee experience, and continuously evolve. Others go live… and stall.

The difference isn’t the technology. It’s everything around it.

It is Not only IT Project that has an end, It is a Business Transformation than doesn’t stop

One of the biggest differentiators is how organizations frame the transformation from day one.

The ones that succeed keep the cloud minsets that should have been infused since the beginning of the implementation. . They don’t ask, “How do we replicate what we had before?” Instead, they ask, “How do we want to work going forward?”

This shift leads to:

  • Simpler, more scalable processes
  • Adoption of standard best practices
  • Faster time to value

In contrast, organizations that try to recreate legacy complexity in a new system often slow themselves down and limit the long-term impact.

Where Transformations Quietly Break: Adoption

We have seen projects that looked perfect on paper, on time, on budget, aligned with best practices, yet struggled immediately after go-live.

In one case, a company implemented core HR and performance management successfully from a technical standpoint. But they underestimated one critical factor: adoption and change management.

The assumption was simple: “The system is intuitive, and people will figure it out.”

They didn’t.

  • Managers avoided using the system or continued working in Excel
  • HR teams were pulled back into manual support and firefighting
  • Data became inconsistent and unreliable
  • Leadership began questioning the value of the investment

Nothing was “wrong” with the system. But the behaviors didn’t change, and without that, transformation simply doesn’t happen.

What Successful Organizations Do Differently

The organizations that truly succeed don’t leave adoption to chance. They are intentional about how people experience the change.

A few patterns consistently stand out:

  1. They invest early in change, not as an afterthought

They engage stakeholders (especially managers) before go-live, communicate the why, and make the change relevant to daily work and keep the effort on as cloud technology is constantly evolving

  1. They focus on practical, role-based continuous enablement

Every person touching SuccessFactors technology should have an adequate enablement, personas need to be defined so the right training at the right time is delivered to all: HR Key Users, HR Operational Users, People Managers and End Users even Deskless Workers could represent a different persona

  1. They build internal champions

They identify trusted voices within the business who can support peers, reinforce the message, and drive momentum locally.

  1. They measure and manage adoption

They track usage, completion rates, and feedback, then act on it. Adoption is actively managed, not assumed.

 

The Role of HR, IT, and Leadership

 

Successful adoption is never accidental. It’s driven by consistent behaviors across three key groups:

HR: From System Owners to Experience Owners

High-performing HR teams go beyond configuration. They design the employee and manager experience, simplify processes, and continuously evolve the system. They also take strong ownership of data quality and governance.

IT: Enablers, Not Gatekeepers

In the cloud world, IT’s role shifts. The most effective teams focus on integration, security, and scalability, all while enabling agility. They collaborate closely with HR rather than controlling every change.

Leadership: Visible and Accountable Champions

This is often the biggest differentiator. Leaders in successful transformations:

  • Actively use the system themselves
  • Reinforce the importance of the change
  • Hold teams accountable for adoption

When leaders lead by example, the organization follows.

Beyond Go-Live: Where Value Is Created

Another key difference is how organizations think about the journey.

Struggling organizations often treat go-live as the finish line.

Successful ones know it’s just the beginning.

They invest in:

  • Continuous improvement
  • Regular review of new releases
  • Ongoing feedback and optimization

They treat the system as a product that evolves, not a project that ends.

Final Thoughts

Cloud HR transformation doesn’t fail because of technology. It fails when organizations treat it like one.

The companies that succeed are the ones that invest just as much in people, behaviors, and mindset as they do in systems.

Because in the end, it’s not implementation that drives value, it’s adoption and ultimately, meaningful results for the organization.

We are trying to test a very simple automation before scheduling it. The automation only executes a local PowerShell script on a Windows VM through Desktop Agent 3. The runtime execution works correctly when we use an Automation Launcher / scheduled execution. The Desktop Agent receives the job and the PowerShell script is executed successfully.

Could you confirm the expected behavior?

Nizare

entry.title: Re: API: Read and Modify WBS element Status
entry.content:
Dear @XANDOGAN,

Classic BAPI-based access such as BAPI_BUS2054_GET_STATUS is not the target design. For WBS elements managed as Enterprise Project elements, SAP provides the released Enterprise Project API technical service API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV_0002. This service supports create, read, update and delete operations for project definitions and project elements however, changing the processing status is handled through the dedicated function import, not by directly patching the status field

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/b467d86283be4a56869f1e6784e47b64.html

https://me.sap.com/notes/3542797

For reading a WBS/project element, the relevant entity is A_EnterpriseProjectElement which can be read using HTTP GET

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/916b8ede65904758a6809596b6af9e41.html

For changing the WBS/project element processing status the correct standard operation is not a direct PATCH of the status field but the function import ChangeEntProjElmntProcgStatus where ProjectElementUUID is passed as the key

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/cdbb18c1e51e43c599e035ee037e6314.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/e3a1e6b27add49ee84c047a10b9ba06e.html

The available processing-status values can be read using the code list service ENTPROJECTPROCESSINGSTATUS / Enterprise Project Read Project Processing Status

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/090091bf7f0e46bcbce23f2ebda8905d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/35f5f46c6bb14edead3851c8ecfde7a0.html

IMPORTANT: This covers the processing status of an enterprise project/WBS element. SAP defines this status as the lifecycle stage of the project or WBS element. It should not be positioned as a full Public Cloud replacement for classic PS system/user status handling via BAPI_BUS2054_GET_STATUS

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/d442899643f342e3af889580cbc2ad32.html

Summary:

Yes, a standard released API exists if the requirement is to read/change WBS processing status use API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV_0002 and ChangeEntProjElmntProcgStatus. If the client expects full classic BAPI-style access to all WBS system/user statuses that is not exposed 1:1 in Public Cloud and the requirement should be narrowed to the supported processing-status model or raised as a feature request

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.content:
Dear Garrett, 

We too are braving this excursion of darkness barefoot and the floor seems to have squishy and sharp objects galore.  Sometimes our well prepped travel plans for such endeavors are met with unilateral enviro deployment/functionality and our preparation is found wanting, as instructions and SAP maps and directives are deficient. We're also having to provide Support with specific approaches and call outs, including in their Provisioned views, to ensure our guides are not consumed by the unexpected darkness and it's pitfalls. Some require we send out search parties in an attempt to retrieve our guides and any Engineering replies. 

We managed a glimpse of the UN issue that correlated with with latest people profile enablement pre New Release. It offered a sliver of light as our integrated creds process was failing to update UNs/logins were failing for New Hires hired after LPP was enabled (despite all correct config and hires pre LPP displayed appropriately).

Sadly, the illuminated view was lost while engaged with Support once New Release was deployed in Preview. We've since taken a blow to the gut - most likely a random kitchen cleaning tool (probably a broom to sweep said puppy surprise under the rug). Although we have gotten some traction from said rug on the apostrophe disaster we called out in business email limitations thanks to the new LPP UI, the UN challenge remains. 

We have been replacing the inactive emp UN with a different emp ID indicator (we have 2 different IDs created for emps upon hire - not including the SAP stipulated thanks to latest ONB. One cred is avail for reuse post term/inactivation of orig holder, and the other is unique for the emp). 

IAS is also leaving the previous holder active in many instances, so I run the update job and make sure it takes before updating the new/active holder with the UN. Any chance that might work for you? 

Sincerely, 

Mandi Arney

Notorious SAP Community Blog Problem Child (and equally disgruntled, but not nearly as eloquent, Sys Admin)
